project.objective: Who were the parents of Adrian Zuñiga Rojas, born 8 September 1889 in Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia?

questions.question: What does the baptismal record for Adrián Zuñiga Rojas, born 8 September 1889 in Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia, record about his parents?
questions.rationale: Catholic parish baptismal registers are the primary vital records for late-19th-century Bolivia. Arani, Cochabamba was an established parish town; records for 1889 are likely to survive and to name the godparents and biological parents. This is the gatekeeper question — finding the baptismal entry would directly answer the objective and determine whether additional corroboration questions are needed.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Although Adrián Zuñiga Rojas's own baptism record was not found in any indexed collection (pli_001 nil, pli_002 produced the 1913 marriage instead), the parentage question is answered by two independent original Catholic parish records from San Bartolomé, Arani: (1) Adrián's 1913 marriage record (src_001, QL7X-VBXX) in which he named his father Pedro Suñiga and mother Polonia Rojas, and (2) the 1887 baptism of his sibling Ramón Suñiga Rojas (src_002, 7KQQ-L5N2) which independently names the same parents from a different informant and event 26 years earlier. The compound surname Suñiga Rojas in both records is consistent with Bolivian naming convention (paternal Suñiga + maternal Rojas). Bolivia Marriages (pli_004) returned no marriage record for the parents, but this is absence of evidence in a partially-indexed collection, not contradicting evidence. Browse-only volumes (pli_003) and Ancestry (pli_005) are documented limitations with explicit rationales in log_009 and log_010.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: YES. Two independent records from San Bartolomé parish, Arani name Pedro Suñiga and Polonia Rojas as parents of children with compound surname Suñiga Rojas. The parentage question is answered.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: YES with documented limitations. FamilySearch collections 1520550, 1922463, and 1520564 all searched. Browse-only San Bartolomé volumes (imageGroupPrefixes 005091539-41) documented as requiring manual browsing (log_009). Ancestry collection 9795 documented as inaccessible due to no subscription (log_010). All accessible digitized sources for this jurisdiction were searched.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: YES. Both src_001 (1913 marriage) and src_002 (1887 Ramón baptism) are original Catholic parish registers accessed via FamilySearch digitization. No derivative-only evidence relied upon.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: YES. src_001 (1913 marriage: Adrián stating his own parentage) and src_002 (1887 baptism: parents presenting Ramón for baptism) are truly independent — different events, different years, different informants, different circumstances. Two distinct informant units per GPS Standard 46.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: YES. src_001 is an original Catholic register with primary information (parties to the marriage self-reported). src_002 is an original Catholic register with primary information (parents presented child for baptism). Both meet the highest evidence-class standard.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: YES. No contradicting evidence was found across any of the 10 logged searches. All evidence agrees on the same parents.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: LOW. Two independent original records from the same parish name the same parents. No search produced evidence pointing to alternative parents. The unsearched avenues (browse-only images, Ancestry) would be most likely to produce Adrián's baptism confirming the same parents, not contradicting them. A finding of different parents would require explaining both the marriage record and the Ramón baptism.

items.rationale: Bolivia, Baptisms, 1560–1938 (collection 1520550) is the primary indexed source for Catholic baptisms in Bolivia. Arani parish (San Bartolomé) records are included; volumes show 93–100% indexed coverage. The baptismal entry for Adrián Zuñiga Rojas on or around 8 September 1889 would name both parents by their full compound surnames — directly answering the research question.

Bolivia, Catholic Church Records, 1566–2020 (collection 1922463) is a broader collection (9.8M records vs 1.87M in the Baptisms-only set) that may contain images or index entries not captured in the dedicated baptisms collection. Search this if pli_001 yields no result — it is the most complete Bolivia church record set on FamilySearch.

Multiple browse-only image groups for San Bartolomé, Arani parish (imageGroupPrefixes 005091539, 005091540, 005091541) exist on FamilySearch with no explicit date range shown in the volume metadata. If the indexed searches (pli_001, pli_002) fail to surface the 1889 baptism, direct image browsing of these volumes may locate the entry. All volumes are 93–100% indexed, but index gaps could cause the record to be missed in keyword search.

Bolivia, Marriages, 1630–1940 (collection 1520564) — searching for the marriage of Adrián's parents (a man surnamed Zuñiga marrying a woman surnamed Rojas in or near Arani, probably 1865–1892). A marriage record would corroborate the parentage found in the baptism and may supply additional identifying details (parents' birthplaces, ages, or their own parents). Also serves as an independent line of evidence in case the baptism is missing or ambiguous.

Ancestry Bolivia Select Baptisms, 1560–1938 (https://www.ancestry.com/search/collections/9795/) indexes the same underlying records as the FamilySearch baptisms collection but uses an independent indexing and name-matching algorithm. Searching Ancestry separately accounts for name-variant matching differences and any records indexed by Ancestry but not by FamilySearch. Use if pli_001 and pli_002 are unproductive.

FAN pivot: siblings of Adrián Zuñiga Rojas baptized in Arani before and after 1889 would share the same parents and corroborate who they were. Searching Bolivia, Baptisms (collection 1520550) for other children with surname Zuñiga Rojas (or Zuñiga + Rojas) in Arani from roughly 1870–1892 tests whether additional records independently name the same father and mother — strengthening the proof argument and potentially surfacing variant spellings.

log.notes: Collection 1520550 (Bolivia, Baptisms) returned zero results for Zuñiga/Adrian with birthplace Arani and birth year 1884–1894. Tilde variant attempted next.

Retry without tilde (Zuniga) and without birthPlace field — still zero results in collection 1520550. The dedicated baptisms collection does not appear to index Arani parish baptisms for 1889 under this name. Proceeding to pli_002 (broader Bolivia, Catholic Church Records collection).

query.note: All Bolivia collections, birth year range

log.notes: 29 matches across Bolivia Catholic Church Records (collection 1922463). Key find: result QL7X-VBXX — marriage record 'Registro para Adrian Suñiga y Gregoria Peñarrieta', 29 Nov 1913, San Bartolomé, Arani — names parents of Adrian as Pedro Suñiga (father) and Polonia Rojas (mother). Separate result QL7X-G24S is a duplicate marriage index entry (no parents). Results QL7X-K1JW, QL7X-KXN9, QL7X-KFZN are children's baptisms already linked in tree. QL7X-GHBV is a child's death record. Q2H1-7LPF is a different Adrian (El Trigal, Santa Cruz). Collection 1520550 not holding these records explains prior nils.
Bolivia Marriages 1520564: Zuñiga × Rojas in Arani 1865–1892 — zero results.
Retry with spelling variant Suñiga — still zero results.

3 results returned (Hilario Zuniga×Maria Velarde 1875 Punata; Amelia Zuniga×Julian Ferrufino 1892 Punata; Juan Suniga×Cirilo Bustamante 1889 Punata). All in Punata, none matching Pedro Zuñiga×Polonia Rojas.

1 result: Pedro Suniga as father of Yldifonza Suniga (spouse Donisia Loaisa), Agustin Duarte marriage 1871 — wrong family, different wife and child. Bolivia Marriages collection 1520564 does not appear to contain Arani parish marriage records for Pedro Zuñiga × Polonia Rojas. Collection may have incomplete coverage for Arani in this period.

Strong corroboration: Ramón Suñiga Rojas baptized 30 Aug 1887 at San Bartolomé, Arani — parents Pedro Suñiga × Polonia Rojas — compound surname Suñiga Rojas confirms naming convention. Also Leonidas Suñiga (born/died 1886, Arani; father Pedro Suñiga, mother Rojas), Benita Zuñiga (1895, Arani; Pedro Zuñiga × Polonia Rojas), Cecilia Zúñiga (1890, Arani; Pedro Zuñiga × Polonia Rojas). Multiple siblings independently name the same parents.

query.purpose: Verify San Bartolomé parish image groups for browse-only 1889 baptism search

log.notes: SKIP RATIONALE (pli_003): volume_search confirms 6 image volumes for San Bartolomé, Arani parish (imageGroupPrefixes 005091539, 005091540, 005091541; ~1,323 images total) but ALL volumes lack explicit date-range metadata and have fulltextSearchable: false. Without date metadata, it is impossible to identify which volume(s) cover 1889 without manually browsing every image. Autonomous research cannot perform undated image browsing. This is a known limitation requiring human researcher access to FamilySearch to manually locate the 1889 baptism page. Recommending this as a follow-up action for the researcher. The existing indexed evidence (1913 marriage + 1887 Ramon baptism) provides a two-record independent basis for the parentage conclusion; image browsing would strengthen it further if Adrian's own baptism entry can be located.

SKIP RATIONALE (pli_005): researcher_profile.subscriptions is empty ([]). No Ancestry subscription is available for this project. Ancestry Bolivia Select Baptisms (collection 9795) cannot be searched without an active subscription. This is a documented access limitation, not a search failure. Recommending as a follow-up action: if the researcher obtains Ancestry access, search for Adrian Zuniga/Suñiga born 1889 in Arani, Cochabamba in collection 9795 as an independent-index check.

assertions.informant_bias_notes: A person cannot provide primary information about their own birth — not cognitively aware at the event. Adrian stated his birth year (1889) at the 1913 marriage, 24 years after the fact. This is a secondhand recollection of what he was told, not first-hand awareness.

assertions.value: married 29 November 1913, San Bartolomé, Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia
assertions.standard_place: San Bartolomé, Arani, Arani, Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia
assertions.informant_bias_notes: The parish officiant performed and witnessed the marriage ceremony, making them a direct witness to the event (date, place, parties). GPS table classifies 'Clergyman' as proximity 'witness' for church marriage registers.

Polonia Rojas named in mother_of_groom role in 1913 marriage record. Forms one evidential unit with a_004/a_005/a_006. NOTE: independently corroborated by Ramon's 1887 baptism (a_017) where Polonia Rojas appears as mother_of_child. Independence gap resolved.

Birth year 'born 1889' matches tree person Adrian Zuñiga Rojas born 8 Sep 1889. Same record as a_001; same_person score 0.9998 establishes the groom is 2DTG-7MK.
Marriage event on 29 Nov 1913 at San Bartolomé, Arani matches the couple relationship in tree.gedcomx.json for 2DTG-7MK and 2DTG-7XD. same_person score 0.9998 for the groom.

The same marriage event bears on the bride Gregoria Peñarrieta Obando (2DTG-7XD). matchRelatives score 0.9999 for the bride persona p_102922351644 matched to 2DTG-7XD. Name, birth year 1893, and place all consistent.

Assertion records Adrian (the groom, 2DTG-7MK) stating his father's name. This assertion bears on Adrian as the child who provided the information. Established identity of groom via same_person score 0.9998.

Pedro Suñiga is named as father of the groom in the 1913 marriage record. This is the sole source introducing this person; no independent corroboration yet. Stub created from this record. Name and relationship role are consistent with the Zuñiga surname pattern (Zuñiga Rojas = Zuñiga father + Rojas mother). Probable pending corroborating evidence.

Assertion records Adrian (the groom, 2DTG-7MK) stating his mother's name. This assertion bears on Adrian as the child providing maternal information. same_person score 0.9998 establishes groom identity.

Polonia Rojas is named as mother of the groom in the 1913 marriage record. The surname 'Rojas' is the second surname component of Adrian Zuñiga Rojas, consistent with Bolivian naming convention (paternal + maternal surname). Stub created from this record; no independent corroboration yet. Probable pending further evidence.

Pedro Suñiga appears in the father_of_groom role in the 1913 marriage record. Name 'Pedro Suñiga' matches the stub I1 introduced by a_004. Same record and informant as a_004 — one evidential unit. Probable as sole-source stub.

Polonia Rojas appears in the mother_of_groom role in the 1913 marriage record. Name 'Polonia Rojas' matches stub I2 introduced by a_005. Same record and informant as a_005 — one evidential unit. Probable as sole-source stub.
Bride name 'Gregoria Peñarrieta' matches tree person Gregoria Peñarrieta Obando (2DTG-7XD). matchRelatives score 0.9999. Name, birth year 1893, and Arani location all consistent.
Bride birth year 'born 1893' matches tree person 2DTG-7XD born 1893. Same record persona as a_008; matchRelatives score 0.9999 establishes bride identity.

Ciriaco Peñarrieta named as father_of_bride (Gregoria Peñarrieta Obando) in the 1913 marriage record. Stub created from this sole source. Consistent with bride's compound surname Peñarrieta Obando (Peñarrieta = father's surname). Probable pending further evidence.

Adriana Obando named as mother_of_bride in the 1913 marriage record. Stub created from this sole source. Consistent with bride's compound surname Peñarrieta Obando (Obando = mother's surname). Probable pending further evidence.

Name 'Ramón Suñiga Rojas' in child role in the 1887 Arani baptism record. Stub I5 introduced from this sole source. Compound surname Suñiga Rojas identifies him as a child of Pedro Suñiga and Polonia Rojas, consistent with Bolivian naming convention.
Baptism event at San Bartolomé, Arani 30 Aug 1887 is the sole dated fact for stub I5. Consistent with a sibling of Adrián born 1889 in the same parish.
Assertion records Ramón (I5) as the child who has father Pedro Suñiga named in this baptism record. Bears on I5 as child.

Pedro Suñiga is named as father in Ramón's 1887 baptism at San Bartolomé, Arani — the same name, spelling, and location as the father named in Adrián's 1913 marriage record (a_004/a_006). Two independent records from the same parish naming the same person as father of children with compound surname Suñiga Rojas. Name, role, place, and time period are all consistent. Confident.
Assertion records Ramón (I5) as the child who has mother Polonia Rojas named in this baptism record. Bears on I5 as child.

Polonia Rojas is named as mother in Ramón's 1887 baptism at San Bartolomé, Arani — the same name and location as the mother named in Adrián's 1913 marriage record (a_005/a_007). Two independent records from the same parish 26 years apart both name Polonia Rojas as mother of Suñiga Rojas children. Confident.

Pedro Suñiga appears as father_of_child in Ramón's 1887 baptism at San Bartolomé, Arani. Second independent record naming this person; first was Adrián's 1913 marriage record (pe_006, pe_009). Name matches exactly. Same spelling, same parish. Corroboration across two records separated by 26 years establishes identity as confident.

Polonia Rojas appears as mother_of_child in Ramón's 1887 baptism at San Bartolomé, Arani. Second independent record naming this person; first was Adrián's 1913 marriage record (pe_008, pe_010). Name matches exactly. Same parish. Corroboration across two records confirms identity as confident.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: FamilySearch Bolivia Baptisms (collection 1520550) searched — nil for Adrián Zuñiga/Suñiga 1889 (log_001, log_002). FamilySearch Bolivia Catholic Church Records (collection 1922463) searched — returned Adrián's 1913 marriage record naming parents Pedro Suñiga and Polonia Rojas (log_003). Bolivia Marriages (collection 1520564) searched with four variant queries for Pedro Zuñiga/Suñiga × Polonia Rojas — nil (log_004–log_007). FAN sibling search in collection 1922463 — found Ramón Suñiga Rojas baptism 1887 naming same parents (log_008). Browse-only San Bartolomé volumes (imageGroupPrefixes 005091539–41, ~1,323 images) confirmed by volume_search but require manual browsing; documented in log_009 as an autonomous-research limitation. Ancestry Bolivia Baptisms (collection 9795) not accessible — no subscription; documented in log_010. Adrián's own baptism record was not found in any indexed collection; the parentage answer comes from his 1913 marriage record and his sibling's 1887 baptism.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## The Parents of Adrián Zuñiga Rojas of Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia

### Conclusion

The evidence establishes that **Adrián Zuñiga Rojas** (born 8 September 1889, Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia; died 21 April 1928, Arani) was the son of **Pedro Suñiga (Zuñiga)** and **Polonia Rojas**, both of Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia.

### Primary Evidence: Adrián's 1913 Marriage Record

The strongest and most direct evidence is Adrián's own Catholic marriage record at San Bartolomé parish, Arani, dated 29 November 1913.¹ In this record the groom is identified as "Adrián Suñiga" (born 1889), and both parents are explicitly named: his father as **Pedro Suñiga** and his mother as **Polonia Rojas**. The bride is Gregoria Peñarrieta (born 1893), daughter of Ciriaco Peñarrieta and Adriana Obando.

This record is an original Catholic parish register (source classification: *original*). The information regarding the parents' names is primary — Adrián, as an adult participant at his own marriage, self-reported his parentage to the recording priest. The evidence is direct: the record explicitly names Pedro Suñiga and Polonia Rojas in the father-of-groom and mother-of-groom roles (assertions a_004–a_007). There is no known incentive for Adrián to misrepresent his parents at his own marriage ceremony.

All four parentage assertions from this record share a single informant (Adrián himself) and therefore constitute one evidential unit per GPS Standard 46 — they receive no more combined weight than the strongest single item.

### Corroborating Evidence: 1887 Baptism of Ramón Suñiga Rojas

Independent corroboration comes from a Catholic baptism record at San Bartolomé parish, Arani, dated 30 August 1887 — twenty-six years before the marriage record and two years before Adrián's birth.² This record documents the baptism of **Ramón Suñiga Rojas**, a male child, naming his father as **Pedro Suñiga** and his mother as **Polonia Rojas** (assertions a_014–a_017).

This source is also an original Catholic parish register. The information is primary — the parent(s) presenting Ramón for baptism reported both parents' names to the priest. The evidence is indirect with respect to Adrián's parentage, requiring the inference that Ramón was Adrián's sibling sharing the same parents. That inference is supported by three converging lines: (1) the same parish and overlapping time period, (2) the compound surname of the baptized child, and (3) Adrián's own compound surname encoding the same parentage (discussed below).

These two records are genuinely independent informant units. The 1913 informant is Adrián himself at his marriage; the 1887 informant is the presenting parent(s) at Ramón's baptism two and a half decades earlier. Different event, different year, different informant circumstances.

### Bolivian Compound-Surname Convention as Corroborating Structure

Under the Catholic Bolivian naming convention of the late nineteenth century, a child's compound surname consists of the father's first (paternal) surname followed by the mother's first (paternal) surname. The name "Adrián Zuñiga Rojas" (or "Suñiga Rojas" in the spelling variant prevalent in Arani records) directly encodes the parentage: Zuñiga/Suñiga from the father, Rojas from the mother. Ramón's full compound surname "Suñiga Rojas" in his 1887 baptism record reflects precisely the same structure. Both the marriage record and the baptism record are therefore internally consistent with each other and with the naming convention.

### Additional Siblings (Supporting Context)

A FAN search of FamilySearch collection 1922463 identified additional apparent children of Pedro Zuñiga/Suñiga × Polonia Rojas recorded at San Bartolomé and Arani: Leonidas Suñiga (born and died 1886, San Bartolomé), Benita Zuñiga (born ~1895, San Bartolomé; died 1921), and Cecilia Zúñiga (born ~1889–1890, Arani). These records were not formally extracted into the evidence base but are consistent with the identified parents and the same parish.

### Negative Evidence and Research Limitations

Adrián's own baptism record was not found in any searched indexed collection. Bolivia Baptisms (FamilySearch collection 1520550) and the broader Bolivia Catholic Church Records (collection 1922463) were both searched without locating a baptism entry for Adrián in 1889 or nearby years. This absence likely reflects an indexing gap rather than the non-existence of the record: browse-only image volumes for San Bartolomé parish exist on FamilySearch (imageGroupPrefixes 005091539–005091541, approximately 1,323 images across six volumes) but require manual browsing, which was not possible in this research session. Locating Adrián's own baptism entry remains a recommended follow-up action.

No marriage record for Pedro Zuñiga × Polonia Rojas was found in Bolivia Marriages (collection 1520564) after four variant searches. This absence does not contradict the parentage conclusion; it may reflect indexing gaps for Arani parish in the relevant period.

No evidence naming different parents for Adrián was found in any of the ten searched and documented sources.

### Summary

Two independent original Catholic parish registers from San Bartolomé, Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia — Adrián's 1913 marriage record and his sibling Ramón's 1887 baptism — both name **Pedro Suñiga (Zuñiga)** and **Polonia Rojas** as the parents of a Suñiga Rojas child from Arani. The compound-surname structure of Adrián's own name is consistent with this parentage. No contradicting evidence was found across ten logged searches. Research was declared reasonably exhaustive.

Documents prove and verify that **Adrián Zuñiga Rojas**, born 8 September 1889 in Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia, was the son of **Pedro Suñiga (Zuñiga)** and **Polonia Rojas** of Arani, Cochabamba, Bolivia.

---

¹ "Bolivia, registros parroquiales, 1566–2020," *FamilySearch* (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:QL7X-VBXX : accessed 2 Jul 2026), entry for Adrián Suñiga and Gregoria Peñarrieta, 29 Nov 1913.

² "Bolivia, registros parroquiales, 1566–2020," *FamilySearch* (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:7KQQ-L5N2 : accessed 2 Jul 2026), entry for Ramón Suñiga Rojas and Pedro Suñiga, 30 Aug 1887.
